operating_state: Morgan Stanley reported net revenues of $17.7 billion in Q1 2025, increasing 17% from the prior year driven by strong performance across all segments. Institutional Securities delivered 28% growth primarily from higher equity trading revenues and gains on corporate loan sales versus prior year mark-to-market losses. Wealth Management grew 6% on higher asset management fees despite lower transactional revenues, while Investment Management increased 16% from higher performance-based income. Trading assets expanded to $400 billion from $332 billion reflecting increased client activity and market positioning.
cost_structure: Total non-interest expenses increased 15% to $12.1 billion primarily from higher compensation costs driven by discretionary incentive compensation on increased revenues and $144 million in severance costs from March workforce reductions affecting approximately 2% of global employees. Non-compensation expenses rose reflecting higher execution-related and distribution expenses. The firm's expense efficiency ratio was 68% for the quarter.
material_events: The firm executed workforce reductions in March 2025 impacting approximately 2% of global employees across all segments related to performance management and workforce alignment. The company repurchased $1.0 billion in common stock with $17.5 billion remaining under authorization. Raja Akram resigned as Deputy CFO and Chief Accounting Officer effective March 26, 2025, with Victoria Worster appointed as replacement effective May 15, 2025.
macro_exposures: Provision for credit losses increased to $135 million from a $6 million benefit in the prior year, reflecting deterioration in macroeconomic outlook incorporated into ACL models with slower economic growth assumptions and higher interest rates. Credit provisions included specific loans related to California wildfires and certain commercial real estate and corporate loans. The firm maintains exposure to variable interest entities with maximum loss exposure of $204.2 billion in unpaid principal balance.
forward_outlook: Management maintains a 20% return on tangible common equity goal based on normal market conditions. The base scenario used in ACL models assumes slower economic growth with forecasted U.S. real GDP growth rates of 1.5% for 4Q 2025 and 1.9% for 4Q 2026. Wealth Management added $94 billion in net new assets with fee-based asset flows indicating continued client acquisition momentum.
uncertainty_profile: The Federal Reserve is expected to publish CCAR and supervisory stress test results by June 30, 2025, with the firm required to disclose company-run stress test results within 15 days thereafter. Significant uncertainty remains regarding global trade policy evolution and impacts on borrowers and counterparties. Level 3 fair value assets increased to $7.8 billion with expanded exposure to illiquid positions, while credit risk-related contingent features in derivative contracts totaled $19.9 billion with potential additional collateral requirements under downgrade scenarios.
